EGLL.notes: 27L/27R preferred when tailwind <=5kt. Westerly config: L/R determined by HAL alternation programme changing at 1500 local — static config cannot track this, both 27s listed. Easterly arrivals always 09L per explicit SOP wording. Night ops single runway, no fixed VATSIM time trigger.

EGKK.source: VATSIM UK London Gatwick vMATS Part 2 Rev 2025/03
EGKK.notes: 26L/08R always primary. 26L preferred calm wind runway when tailwind <=5kt. Northern runway 26R/08L explicitly not recommended for VATSIM use per SOP — excluded from config. Effectively single-runway airport on VATSIM.

EGSS.notes: RWY 22 preferred when tailwind <=5kt and surface dry. Calm/changing wind SOP requires TAF and 2000ft wind — unavailable to this system. Single runway, no alternation complexity.

EGLC.notes: 27 stated preferred but SOP explicitly requires most into-wind runway due to BAe146/RJ zero tailwind tolerance. Calm wind SOP requires 2000ft wind consideration — unavailable to this system. no_factor_wind reduced to 3kt reflecting tighter operational requirements. 27 listed first as documented preference only.

EGCC.source: VATSIM UK Manchester vMATS Part 2 Rev 2025/02
EGCC.notes: 23L/23R preferred when tailwind <=5kt. Dual westerly: 23L dep, 23R arr. Dual easterly: 05L dep, 05R arr. Single runway uses 05L/23R combined. 05R/23L secondary — only when 05L/23R closed. Dual/single switching is time and traffic dependent.

LSZH.source: Swiss Virtual Airlines Operations Manual C
LSZH.notes: Three time-based configs. General/daytime: arr 14, dep 28 (tailwind tolerance 10kt on 14, 7kt on 28). Morning: arr 34, dep 32. Evening: arr 28, dep 32. VATSIM SOP notes Tower ATC may not comply with time-based procedures during peak evening traffic. no_factor_wind set to 7kt reflecting RWY28 documented tailwind tolerance.

LSGG.source: Swiss Virtual Airlines Operations Manual C
LSGG.notes: RWY 22 preferred when tailwind <=4kt. RWY 04 used when tailwind exceeds 4kt. Single runway, no alternation complexity. no_factor_wind set to 4kt matching documented threshold precisely.

ESSA.notes: Peak config: 01R arr + 01L dep, or 19L arr + 19R dep. Off-peak has six documented combinations. RWY08 arrivals and RWY26 departures explicitly last resort only. Night: 19R dep only for performance reasons 2200-0700 local.

EDDF.source: VATSIM community guide — NOT official VACC-GER vMATS
EDDF.notes: MEDIUM CONFIDENCE — no official vACC SOP located. West ops (winds 200-300deg): arr 25L+25R, dep 25C+18. East ops (winds 030-120deg): arr 07L+07C, dep 07R+07C. RWY18 exclusively for departures, typically southbound. Update if official VACC-GER documentation is found.

LFPG.notes: Outer runways 08R/26L and 09L/27R preferred for arrivals. Inner runways 08L/26R and 09R/27L preferred for departures. Inner runway ILS physically switched off except LVP — arrivals on outers is a structural constraint. 26R restricted to chapter 3 westbound/left-turn departures except when 27L unserviceable.

RJTT.notes: MEDIUM CONFIDENCE. North wind daytime (1400-2100Z): dep 05, arr 34R. North wind night: dep 34R, arr 34L+34R. South wind daytime: dep 16L, arr 23. South wind night: dep 16L/R, arr 22+23. International flights 1400-2100Z: 16L/34R both movements. RWY04 last resort only.
